zh.Ysceifiog, Flintshire, Wales, United Kin: 英國威爾斯福林爾易費格
zh.Ysceifiog, Flintshire, Wales: 英國威爾斯福林爾易費格
zh.lifeSummary: 當Hywel "Wynne" ab Edward ap Badi於1530年在英國威爾斯福林爾易費格受洗時，他的父親Edward ap Badi23歲，母親Katherine Verch Ithel18歲。他於1553年與Jane Griffith在威爾士福林爾弗林特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1583年在英國威爾斯福林爾享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hywel "Wynne" ab Edward ap Badi于1530年在英国威尔斯福林爾易費格受洗时，他的父亲Edward ap Badi23岁，母亲Katherine Verch Ithel18岁。他于1553年与Jane Griffith在威尔士福林爾弗林特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1583年在英国威尔斯福林爾享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hywel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a vocabulary word meaning ‘eminent, conspicuous’; it was common in the Middle Ages and was revived in the 20th-century.

variants.name: Hywell
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a vocabulary word meaning ‘eminent, conspicuous’; it was common in the Middle Ages and was revived in the 20th-century.

variants.name: Howell
meanings: Anglicized form of the Welsh name Hywel , or a transferred use of the surname derived from that name.

nameMeanings.name: "Wynne"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably a transferred use of the surname, which is derived from the Old and Middle English personal name Wine ‘friend’. See also Wyn (of which this is an elaborated form) .

variants.name: Wynn
meanings: Probably a transferred use of the surname, which is derived from the Old and Middle English personal name Wine ‘friend’. See also Wyn (of which this is an elaborated form) .

nameMeanings.name: ab
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eduardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eideard
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Edward .

variants.name: Édouard
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Edvard

en.lifeSummary: When Hywel "Wynne" ab Edward ap Badi was christened in 1530, in Ysceifiog, Flintshire, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Edward ap Badi, was 23 and his mother, Katherine Verch Ithel, was 18. He married Jane Griffith in 1553, in Flint, Flintshire, Wales. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in 1583, in Flintshire, Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hywel "Wynne" ab Edward ap Badi a été baptisé en 1530, à Ysceifiog, Flintshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Edward ap Badi, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Katherine Verch Ithel, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Jane Griffith en 1553, à Flint, Flintshire, Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1583, à Flintshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hywel "Wynne" ab Edward ap Badi 1530 in Ysceifiog, Flintshire, Wales, United Kingdom getauft wurde, war sein Vater Edward ap Badi 23 und seine Mutter Katherine Verch Ithel 18. Er hat 1553 in Flint, Flintshire, Wales Jane Griffith geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1583 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Flintshire, Wales, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Hywel "Wynne" ab Edward ap Badiが1530年にイギリスウェールズフリントシュアイアイスセイフィオグで洗礼を受けた時，父Edward ap Badiは23歳，母Katherine Verch Ithelは18歳であった。1553年にウェールズフリントシュアイアフリントでJane Griffithと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1583年にイギリスウェールズフリントシュアイアで53歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hywel "Wynne" ab Edward ap Badi(이)가 1530년 영국 웨일스 플린츠히르 이스스이피오그에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward ap Badi(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Katherine Verch Ithel(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1553년 웨일스 플린츠히르 플린트에서 Jane Griffith(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1583년 영국 웨일스 플린츠히르에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hywel "Wynne" ab Edward ap Badi был крещен в 1530 г. в местности иссифиог, флинцхир, Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Edward ap Badi было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Katherine Verch Ithel, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Jane Griffith в 1553 г. в местности флинт, флинцхир, Уэльс. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1583 г. в местности флинцхир, Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 53 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hywel "Wynne" ab Edward ap Badi se bautizó en 1530, en Ysceifiog, Flintshire, Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Edward ap Badi, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Katherine Verch Ithel, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Jane Griffith en 1553, en Flint, Flintshire, Gales. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1583, en Flintshire, Gales, Reino Unido, a la edad de 53 años.
